text: [16] “For God so loved the world, that he gave his only Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish but have eternal life.
[11] For I know the plans I have for you, declares the LORD, plans for welfare and not for evil, to give you a future and a hope.
[13] I can do all things through him who strengthens me.
[28] And we know that for those who love God all things work together for good, for those who are called according to his purpose.

A Psalm of David.

[1] The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.
[2] He makes me lie down in green pastures.
He leads me beside still waters.
[3] He restores my soul.
He leads me in paths of righteousness
for his name’s sake.


[4] Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death,
I will fear no evil,
for you are with me;
your rod and your staff,
they comfort me.


[5] You prepare a table before me
in the presence of my enemies;
you anoint my head with oil;
my cup overflows.
[6] Surely goodness and mercy shall follow me
all the days of my life,
and I shall dwell in the house of the LORD
forever.

[5] Trust in the LORD with all your heart,
and do not lean on your own understanding.
[6] In all your ways acknowledge him,
and he will make straight your paths.
[2] Do not be conformed to this world, but be transformed by the renewal of your mind, that by testing you may discern what is the will of God, what is good and acceptable and perfect.

[6] do not be anxious about anything, but in everything by prayer and supplication with thanksgiving let your requests be made known to God. [7] And the peace of God, which surpasses all understanding, will guard your hearts and your minds in Christ Jesus.
[9] Have I not commanded you? Be strong and courageous. Do not be frightened, and do not be dismayed, for the LORD your God is with you wherever you go.”

[28] Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest. [29] Take my yoke upon you, and learn from me, for I am gentle and lowly in heart, and you will find rest for your souls. [30] For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.”

To the choirmaster. Of the Sons of Korah. According to Alamoth. A Song.

[1] God is our refuge and strength,
a very present help in trouble.
[22] But the fruit of the Spirit is love, joy, peace, patience, kindness, goodness, faithfulness, [23] gentleness, self-control; against such things there is no law.

[38] For I am sure that neither death nor life, nor angels nor rulers, nor things present nor things to come, nor powers, [39] nor height nor depth, nor anything else in all creation, will be able to separate us from the love of God in Christ Jesus our Lord.
[7] for God gave us a spirit not of fear but of power and love and self-control.

[4] Love is patient and kind; love does not envy or boast; it is not arrogant [5] or rude. It does not insist on its own way; it is not irritable or resentful; [6] it does not rejoice at wrongdoing, but rejoices with the truth. [7] Love bears all things, believes all things, hopes all things, endures all things.
[8] For by grace you have been saved through faith. And this is not your own doing; it is the gift of God, [9] not a result of works, so that no one may boast.
[1] Now faith is the assurance of things hoped for, the conviction of things not seen.

[4] Delight yourself in the LORD,
and he will give you the desires of your heart.
[33] But seek first the kingdom of God and his righteousness, and all these things will be added to you.
[6] Jesus said to him, “I am the way, and the truth, and the life. No one comes to the Father except through me.
[8] but God shows his love for us in that while we were still sinners, Christ died for us.

[24] This is the day that the LORD has made;
let us rejoice and be glad in it.
[10] The thief comes only to steal and kill and destroy. I came that they may have life and have it abundantly.
[17] Therefore, if anyone is in Christ, he is a new creation. The old has passed away; behold, the new has come.

Nun

[105] Your word is a lamp to my feet
and a light to my path.
[13] May the God of hope fill you with all joy and peace in believing, so that by the power of the Holy Spirit you may abound in hope.
[7] casting all your anxieties on him, because he cares for you.

[2] Count it all joy, my brothers, when you meet trials of various kinds, [3] for you know that the testing of your faith produces steadfastness. [4] And let steadfastness have its full effect, that you may be perfect and complete, lacking in nothing.
[33] I have said these things to you, that in me you may have peace. In the world you will have tribulation. But take heart; I have overcome the world.”
[1] I appeal to you therefore, brothers, by the mercies of God, to present your bodies as a living sacrifice, holy and acceptable to God, which is your spiritual worship.

[18] The LORD is near to the brokenhearted
and saves the crushed in spirit.

[1] Therefore, since we are surrounded by so great a cloud of witnesses, let us also lay aside every weight, and sin which clings so closely, and let us run with endurance the race that is set before us, [2] looking to Jesus, the founder and perfecter of our faith, who for the joy that was set before him endured the cross, despising the shame, and is seated at the right hand of the throne of God.

[19] Go therefore and make disciples of all nations, baptizing them in the name of the Father and of the Son and of the Holy Spirit, [20] teaching them to observe all that I have commanded you. And behold, I am with you always, to the end of the age.”
[23] Whatever you do, work heartily, as for the Lord and not for men,

[6] Train up a child in the way he should go;
even when he is old he will not depart from it.
[27] Peace I leave with you; my peace I give to you. Not as the world gives do I give to you. Let not your hearts be troubled, neither let them be afraid.
[16] In the same way, let your light shine before others, so that they may see your good works and give glory to your Father who is in heaven.

[9] because, if you confess with your mouth that Jesus is Lord and believe in your heart that God raised him from the dead, you will be saved. [10] For with the heart one believes and is justified, and with the mouth one confesses and is saved.

[1] He who dwells in the shelter of the Most High
will abide in the shadow of the Almighty.
[2] I will say to the LORD, “My refuge and my fortress,
my God, in whom I trust.”
[10] Finally, be strong in the Lord and in the strength of his might. [11] Put on the whole armor of God, that you may be able to stand against the schemes of the devil.
[19] We love because he first loved us.
[7] Submit yourselves therefore to God. Resist the devil, and he will flee from you.

[10] “Be still, and know that I am God.
I will be exalted among the nations,
I will be exalted in the earth!”

[3] Commit your work to the LORD,
and your plans will be established.

[8] He has told you, O man, what is good;
and what does the LORD require of you
but to do justice, and to love kindness,
and to walk humbly with your God?
[1] In the beginning was the Word, and the Word was with God, and the Word was God.
[1] In the beginning, God created the heavens and the earth.

[4] Even though I walk through the valley of the shadow of death,
I will fear no evil,
for you are with me;
your rod and your staff,
they comfort me.
[7] “Ask, and it will be given to you; seek, and you will find; knock, and it will be opened to you.
[23] For the wages of sin is death, but the free gift of God is eternal life in Christ Jesus our Lord.
[17] For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but in order that the world might be saved through him.
[5] Keep your life free from love of money, and be content with what you have, for he has said, “I will never leave you nor forsake you.”

A Song of Ascents.

[1] I lift up my eyes to the hills.
From where does my help come?
[2] My help comes from the LORD,
who made heaven and earth.

[3] You keep him in perfect peace
whose mind is stayed on you,
because he trusts in you.
[2] Set your minds on things that are above, not on things that are on earth.

[10] The name of the LORD is a strong tower;
the righteous man runs into it and is safe.
[16] Rejoice always, [17] pray without ceasing, [18] give thanks in all circumstances; for this is the will of God in Christ Jesus for you.
[34] “Therefore do not be anxious about tomorrow, for tomorrow will be anxious for itself. Sufficient for the day is its own trouble.
[5] If any of you lacks wisdom, let him ask God, who gives generously to all without reproach, and it will be given him.
[5] I am the vine; you are the branches. Whoever abides in me and I in him, he it is that bears much fruit, for apart from me you can do nothing.

[9] But he said to me, “My grace is sufficient for you, for my power is made perfect in weakness.” Therefore I will boast all the more gladly of my weaknesses, so that the power of Christ may rest upon me.
[1] There is therefore now no condemnation for those who are in Christ Jesus.

[10] Create in me a clean heart, O God,
and renew a right spirit within me.
[20] Now to him who is able to do far more abundantly than all that we ask or think, according to the power at work within us,

[37] And he said to him, “You shall love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind. [38] This is the great and first commandment. [39] And a second is like it: You shall love your neighbor as yourself.

[13] No temptation has overtaken you that is not common to man. God is faithful, and he will not let you be tempted beyond your ability, but with the temptation he will also provide the way of escape, that you may be able to endure it.

Of David.

[1] Bless the LORD, O my soul,
and all that is within me,
bless his holy name!
[2] Bless the LORD, O my soul,
and forget not all his benefits,
[3] who forgives all your iniquity,
who heals all your diseases,
[4] who redeems your life from the pit,
who crowns you with steadfast love and mercy,
[5] who satisfies you with good
so that your youth is renewed like the eagle’s.
[24] Therefore I tell you, whatever you ask in prayer, believe that you have received it, and it will be yours.
[32] and you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”
[31] What then shall we say to these things? If God is for us, who can be against us?

[14] Let the words of my mouth and the meditation of my heart
be acceptable in your sight,
O LORD, my rock and my redeemer.

[23] Keep your heart with all vigilance,
for from it flow the springs of life.
[14] “You are the light of the world. A city set on a hill cannot be hidden.
[9] If we confess our sins, he is faithful and just to forgive us our sins and to cleanse us from all unrighteousness.

To the choirmaster: according to Jeduthun. A Psalm of David.

[1] For God alone my soul waits in silence;
from him comes my salvation.
[2] He alone is my rock and my salvation,
my fortress; I shall not be greatly shaken.
[16] Let us then with confidence draw near to the throne of grace, that we may receive mercy and find grace to help in time of need.

[20] I have been crucified with Christ. It is no longer I who live, but Christ who lives in me. And the life I now live in the flesh I live by faith in the Son of God, who loved me and gave himself for me.
[1] “Let not your hearts be troubled. Believe in God; believe also in me.
[23] for all have sinned and fall short of the glory of God,

[8] Oh, taste and see that the LORD is good!
Blessed is the man who takes refuge in him!

[25] Strength and dignity are her clothing,
and she laughs at the time to come.

[8] For my thoughts are not your thoughts,
neither are your ways my ways, declares the LORD.
[9] For as the heavens are higher than the earth,
so are my ways higher than your ways
and my thoughts than your thoughts.
[26] But Jesus looked at them and said, “With man this is impossible, but with God all things are possible.”
[14] Let all that you do be done in love.

[3] He heals the brokenhearted
and binds up their wounds.
[32] Be kind to one another, tenderhearted, forgiving one another, as God in Christ forgave you.
[14] if my people who are called by my name humble themselves, and pray and seek my face and turn from their wicked ways, then I will hear from heaven and will forgive their sin and heal their land.

[34] A new commandment I give to you, that you love one another: just as I have loved you, you also are to love one another. [35] By this all people will know that you are my disciples, if you have love for one another.”
[12] Rejoice in hope, be patient in tribulation, be constant in prayer.

A Psalm for giving thanks.

[1] Make a joyful noise to the LORD, all the earth!
[2] Serve the LORD with gladness!
Come into his presence with singing!


[3] Know that the LORD, he is God!
It is he who made us, and we are his;
we are his people, and the sheep of his pasture.


[4] Enter his gates with thanksgiving,
and his courts with praise!
Give thanks to him; bless his name!


[5] For the LORD is good;
his steadfast love endures forever,
and his faithfulness to all generations.

[25] Whoever brings blessing will be enriched,
and one who waters will himself be watered.

[17] no weapon that is fashioned against you shall succeed,
and you shall refute every tongue that rises against you in judgment.
This is the heritage of the servants of the LORD
and their vindication from me, declares the LORD.”
[28] Come to me, all who labor and are heavy laden, and I will give you rest.
[15] but in your hearts honor Christ the Lord as holy, always being prepared to make a defense to anyone who asks you for a reason for the hope that is in you; yet do it with gentleness and respect,

[5] Commit your way to the LORD;
trust in him, and he will act.
[6] And without faith it is impossible to please him, for whoever would draw near to God must believe that he exists and that he rewards those who seek him.
[9] And let us not grow weary of doing good, for in due season we will reap, if we do not give up.
[12] Again Jesus spoke to them, saying, “I am the light of the world. Whoever follows me will not walk in darkness, but will have the light of life.”
[18] For I consider that the sufferings of this present time are not worth comparing with the glory that is to be revealed to us.

[4] One thing have I asked of the LORD,
that will I seek after:
that I may dwell in the house of the LORD
all the days of my life,
to gaze upon the beauty of the LORD
and to inquire in his temple.

[17] Iron sharpens iron,
and one man sharpens another.

[29] He gives power to the faint,
and to him who has no might he increases strength.

[25] “Therefore I tell you, do not be anxious about your life, what you will eat or what you will drink, nor about your body, what you will put on. Is not life more than food, and the body more than clothing? [26] Look at the birds of the air: they neither sow nor reap nor gather into barns, and yet your heavenly Father feeds them. Are you not of more value than they?
[7] Beloved, let us love one another, for love is from God, and whoever loves has been born of God and knows God. [8] Anyone who does not love does not know God, because God is love.

[3] When I am afraid,
I put my trust in you.
[10] For we are his workmanship, created in Christ Jesus for good works, which God prepared beforehand, that we should walk in them.
[6] Be strong and courageous. Do not fear or be in dread of them, for it is the LORD your God who goes with you. He will not leave you or forsake you.”
[13] Greater love has no one than this, that someone lay down his life for his friends.
[21] Do not be overcome by evil, but overcome evil with good.

[11] For he will command his angels concerning you
to guard you in all your ways.
[44] But I say to you, Love your enemies and pray for those who persecute you,
[58] Therefore, my beloved brothers, be steadfast, immovable, always abounding in the work of the Lord, knowing that in the Lord your labor is not in vain.

but God is the strength of my heart and my portion forever.

[10] And those who know your name put their trust in you,
for you, O LORD, have not forsaken those who seek you.
[12] “So whatever you wish that others would do to you, do also to them, for this is the Law and the Prophets.

[16] So we do not lose heart. Though our outer self is wasting away, our inner self is being renewed day by day. [17] For this light momentary affliction is preparing for us an eternal weight of glory beyond all comparison, [18] as we look not to the things that are seen but to the things that are unseen. For the things that are seen are transient, but the things that are unseen are eternal.

[8] Let me hear in the morning of your steadfast love,
for in you I trust.

for to you I lift up my soul.

and he will sustain you;
he will never permit
the righteous to be moved.

[29] Whoever is slow to anger has great understanding,
but he who has a hasty temper exalts folly.

that she should have no compassion on the son of her womb?
Even these may forget,
yet I will not forget you.

your walls are continually before me.

and why are you in turmoil within me?
Hope in God; for I shall again praise him,
my salvation and my God.

but a harsh word stirs up anger.

[5] God is in the midst of her; she shall not be moved;
God will help her when morning dawns.

[21] Many are the plans in the mind of a man,

hold your right hand;

I am the one who helps you.”

[13] So now faith, hope, and love abide, these three; but the greatest of these is love.

To the choirmaster. A Psalm of David.

[1] I waited patiently for the LORD;
he inclined to me and heard my cry.
[2] He drew me up from the pit of destruction,
out of the miry bog,
and set my feet upon a rock,
making my steps secure.
[3] He put a new song in my mouth,
a song of praise to our God.
Many will see and fear,
and put their trust in the LORD.
[2] with all humility and gentleness, with patience, bearing with one another in love, [3] eager to maintain the unity of the Spirit in the bond of peace.
[8] It is the LORD who goes before you. He will be with you; he will not leave you or forsake you. Do not fear or be dismayed.”
[15] “If you love me, you will keep my commandments.
[8] For if we live, we live to the Lord, and if we die, we die to the Lord. So then, whether we live or whether we die, we are the Lord’s.

[7] Be not wise in your own eyes;
fear the LORD, and turn away from evil.
[8] It will be healing to your flesh
and refreshment to your bones.
[21] And your ears shall hear a word behind you, saying, “This is the way, walk in it,” when you turn to the right or when you turn to the left.
[28] And do not fear those who kill the body but cannot kill the soul. Rather fear him who can destroy both soul and body in hell.
[18] Little children, let us not love in word or talk but in deed and in truth.

[4] For the word of the LORD is upright,
and all his work is done in faithfulness.

[37] All that the Father gives me will come to me, and whoever comes to me I will never cast out.
[37] No, in all these things we are more than conquerors through him who loved us.

A Psalm of David.

[1] The LORD is my shepherd; I shall not want.

[9] The heart of man plans his way,
but the LORD establishes his steps.

[8] The grass withers, the flower fades,
but the word of our God will stand forever.

slow to anger and abounding in steadfast love and faithfulness.

my God, my rock, in whom I take refuge,
my shield, and the horn of my salvation, my stronghold.

to us a son is given;

By guarding it according to your word.

the God of Jacob is our fortress. Selah

all you who wait for the LORD!

so far does he remove our transgressions from us.
